The project is divided into 5 phases with man-hours following a geometric progression and a common ratio of 2. The total man-hours for the project is 310. To find the man-hours for each phase, use the formula for the sum of a geometric series:

Sum = a(1 - r^n)/(1 - r)

Where a is the first term, r is the common ratio, and n is the number of terms. Substituting the known values:

310 = a(1 - 2^5)/(1 - 2)

Solving this equation gives the value of a. The man-hours for each phase can be calculated by multiplying a by the respective powers of 2.

The initial total project duration is 20 weeks. Each phase will now take 1.5 times longer than planned. The new total project duration is calculated by multiplying the original duration by 1.5. This accounts for the increased time required for each phase without altering the sequence of the phases.

The project manager has ensured that all teams are aligned with the updated timeline. The resource allocation plan remains unchanged to maintain efficiency across all phases. The project manager monitors progress closely to ensure that the extended timeline does not lead to delays in subsequent phases.

The project manager has established a structured framework for communication between teams. This ensures that all stakeholders are aware of the updated timeline and their respective responsibilities. The project manager has also set up regular check-ins to review the progress of each phase.

The project manager has implemented a system to track the man-hours spent on each phase. This allows for real-time adjustments to be made if any phase is taking longer than expected. The project manager has also allocated additional resources to phases that are experiencing delays.

The project manager has ensured that the team is fully informed about the changes to the timeline. This transparency helps maintain morale and ensures that all team members understand the new expectations. The project manager has also emphasized the importance of meeting the updated deadlines.

The project manager has reviewed the resource allocation plan to ensure that it remains effective under the new timeline. This includes adjusting the number of team members assigned to each phase as needed. The project manager has also considered the impact of the extended timeline on the overall project budget.

The project manager has established a contingency plan to address any further complications that may arise. This plan includes additional time buffers for each phase to accommodate unexpected delays. The project manager has also reviewed the risk management strategy to ensure it remains relevant.
